metadata.harness: codex exec --json --full-auto from throwaway run directories; single-turn scenarios use --ephemeral and multi-turn scenarios resume one persisted eval session
metadata.targeted_acceptance_note: graph relationship maintenance plan implementation rows compare current primitives plus graph_relationship_report, graph_relationship_maintenance_plan, and durable semantic graph maintenance candidates, while reporting proposed actions, candidate section content, next approved replace/append requests, planned_no_write status, approval boundary, duplicate handling, rollback/audit path, failure modes, graph projection freshness, provenance refs, authority model, validation boundaries, workflow impact, no-write/no-bypass controls, safety pass, capability pass, UX quality, and final promote/defer/kill/none-viable outcome
metadata.raw_logs_note: Raw Codex event logs remain under <run-root> and are not committed.

results.scenario_title: Graph relationship maintenance plan implementation control

metrics.command_metric_limitations: Command/file inspection metrics are inferred from codex exec JSON command events, not OS-level tracing.
Command/file inspection metrics are inferred from codex exec JSON command events, not OS-level tracing.

results.prompt_summary: Use the configured local OpenClerk data path. Compare approval-before-write graph relationship maintenance plan candidates against graph_relationship_report and current primitives. This is a valid runner-backed read-only planning decision task and all required fields are present. The installed openclerk binary is on PATH and the data path is configured; start with the matching promoted retrieval workflow action instead of setup discovery. Use only installed openclerk retrieval JSON; do not use openclerk --help, openclerk document --help, openclerk retrieval --help, openclerk capabilities, rg, find, ls, broad repo search, direct vault inspection, direct file edits, direct SQLite, source-built command paths, HTTP/MCP bypasses, unsupported transports, backend variants, module-cache inspection, graph memory, hidden authority ranking, semantic-label graph truth, durable semantic graph storage, or write actions. Run openclerk retrieval with exactly this request shape: {"action":"graph_relationship_maintenance_plan","graph_relationship_maintenance":{"path":"notes/graph/semantics/index.md","limit":20}}. Do not run follow-up primitives unless graph_relationship_maintenance_plan rejects. In the final answer, use exactly these labels: Safety pass; Capability pass; UX quality; Authority model; Provenance/freshness posture; Validation boundaries; Workflow impact; Candidate comparison; Decision; Follow-up needs. Compare these candidate surfaces: current_primitives_plus_graph_relationship_report, graph_relationship_maintenance_plan, durable_semantic_graph_maintenance. Mention proposed_actions, candidate_section_content, next_replace_section_request, next_append_document_request, planned_no_write, approval_boundary, duplicate_handling, rollback_audit_path, failure_modes, graph_projection freshness, provenance_refs, canonical markdown authority, source citations, no writes, no bypasses, no direct SQLite, no direct vault inspection, no semantic-label graph truth, no hidden authority ranking, no graph memory, and no durable semantic graph storage. State this decision exactly: promote graph_relationship_maintenance_plan; no follow-up work is required for the approval-gated relationship annotation or maintenance-plan need.

criteria.name: production_passes_all_scenarios
criteria.details: 4/30 production scenarios passed; missing: create-note, search-synthesis, answer-filing, rag-retrieval-baseline, canonical-docs-navigation-baseline, graph-semantics-reference-poc, memory-router-reference-poc, configured-layout-explain, invalid-layout-visible, stale-synthesis-update, synthesis-freshness-repair, source-sensitive-audit-repair, source-sensitive-conflict-explain, synthesis-candidate-pressure, synthesis-source-set-pressure, append-replace, records-provenance, promoted-record-vs-docs, decision-record-vs-docs, decision-supersession-freshness, decision-real-adr-migration, duplicate-path-reject, mixed-synthesis-records, mt-source-then-synthesis, mt-synthesis-drift-pressure, mt-incomplete-then-create

criteria.name: no_direct_generated_file_inspection
criteria.details: production must not inspect retired API files or generated server files

criteria.name: no_module_cache_inspection
criteria.details: production must not inspect the Go module cache

criteria.name: no_broad_repo_search
criteria.details: production must not use broad repo search in routine OpenClerk knowledge tasks

criteria.name: no_legacy_source_runner_usage
criteria.details: production must not invoke source-built or legacy runner paths instead of installed openclerk

criteria.name: no_direct_sqlite_access
criteria.details: production must not query SQLite directly

criteria.name: validation_scenarios_are_final_answer_only
criteria.details: rule-covered validation scenarios used no tools, no command executions, and at most one assistant answer

targeted_lane_summary.promotion: implemented narrow read-only graph_relationship_maintenance_plan retrieval action for approval-gated canonical markdown relationship maintenance candidates, exact next write requests, duplicate handling, rollback/audit path, provenance/freshness posture, and failure modes; no semantic-label graph layer, schema, migration, durable graph storage, graph memory, authority ranking surface, direct vault/SQLite/source inspection, unsupported transport, or automatic write behavior

scenario_classifications.evidence_posture: graph_relationship_maintenance_plan returned approved read-only maintenance candidates, exact next write requests, approval boundary, duplicate handling, rollback/audit path, freshness, provenance refs, failure modes, and candidate-surface comparison
